subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Region R — Area, Cross-Section Volume & Rotation about y=6
stimulus: Let $f$ and $g$ be the functions defined by $g(x) = -2 + 3\cos\left(\frac{\pi x}{2}\right)$ and $h(x) = 6 - 2(x-1)^2 - 2\sqrt{|x|}$. Let $R$ be the region enclosed by the graphs of $g$ and $h$. The graphs intersect at $x = -1$ and $x = 2.414$.

parts.prompt: Write, but do not evaluate, an integral expression that gives the area of region $R$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct integrand: $h(x) - g(x)$ (h is on top in region R)
1 pt: Correct limits and expression: $\int_{-1}^{2.414} \left[h(x) - g(x)\right]\, dx$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Area $= \int_{-1}^{2.414} [h(x) - g(x)]\, dx$. Must identify $h$ as the top function (greater values) over $[-1, 2.414]$. Accept $2.414$ or exact equivalent for upper limit.

parts.prompt: Region $R$ is the base of a solid. For this solid, each cross section perpendicular to the $x$-axis is a square with one side in region $R$. Write, but do not evaluate, an integral expression that gives the volume of the solid.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct cross-sectional area: side length = $h(x) - g(x)$, so area of square cross section = $[h(x) - g(x)]^2$
1 pt: Correct integral expression: $\int_{-1}^{2.414} \left[h(x) - g(x)\right]^2\, dx$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Volume $= \int_{-1}^{2.414} [h(x) - g(x)]^2\, dx$. Cross section area = (side)² where side = $h(x) - g(x)$.

parts.prompt: Write, but do not evaluate, an integral expression that gives the volume of the solid generated when region $R$ is rotated about the horizontal line $y = 6$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Uses washer method with outer radius $= 6 - g(x)$ and inner radius $= 6 - h(x)$
1 pt: Correct integral expression: $\pi \int_{-1}^{2.414} \left[(6 - g(x))^2 - (6 - h(x))^2\right]\, dx$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Since $y = 6$ is above the region, outer radius $= 6 - g(x)$ (distance from line to lower curve) and inner radius $= 6 - h(x)$ (distance from line to upper curve). Volume $= \pi \int_{-1}^{2.414} [(6-g(x))^2 - (6-h(x))^2]\, dx$.

parts.prompt: The function $k$ is defined by $k(x) = \frac{g(x)}{h(x)}$. Find the value of $k'(-1)$.
